purpose: Learned state for the paper-trading system. The daily trader READS this before trading. The weekly review WRITES to it. Bounded self-adjustment only.

adjustment_limits.note: The review may pause a source (multiplier 0) or scale it up to 2x base size. It may NEVER change hard safety rules, exceed cash reserve limits, or enable real-money trading.

Berkshire Hathaway.notes: Low turnover - best suited to 13F lag

Scion Asset Management.notes: High turnover - watch whether lag kills the signal

Duquesne Family Office.notes: High turnover - watch whether lag kills the signal

macro_rules.risk_off_definition: VIX > 25, OR major new war/escalation involving a G20 economy or oil region, OR US market circuit-breaker event, OR Fed emergency action
macro_rules.risk_off_action: Halve all new buy sizes; skip buys in directly-affected sectors; never forced to sell
macro_rules.extreme_risk_off_definition: VIX > 35 or market-wide crash day (SPY down >4%)
macro_rules.extreme_risk_off_action: No new buys today; log signals for later review
macro_rules.sector_news_veto: If the ticker being bought has major negative company-specific news today (fraud, guidance cut, regulatory action), skip and log the veto

quality_gate.philosophy: Buffett-style: only buy businesses, not tickers. Every buy candidate must pass a fundamentals check.

checks.dividend: Pays a dividend or does buybacks (score 1, bonus not requirement)
checks.debt: Debt/equity below ~1.5 or investment-grade balance sheet (score 2)
checks.cash_on_hand: Total cash covers short-term debt, or current ratio > 1 (score 2)
checks.receivables_quality: Red flag if accounts receivable growing much faster than revenue (earnings quality warning, score -2 if triggered)

quality_gate.exemption: Signals from low-turnover managers (Berkshire, and any manager the review marks 'trusted') pass automatically - they already did deeper analysis than we can
quality_gate.action_on_fail: Skip the buy, log the score breakdown as QUALITY_VETO

investment_committee.purpose: Every buy that passes the quality gate is debated through six lenses. Each lens gives APPROVE / REJECT / ABSTAIN with one line of reasoning. Need net-positive votes (approvals > rejections) to execute. All votes logged.

members.Warren Buffett: Is this a wonderful business at a fair price? Does it have a moat (pricing power, brand, network effects)? Would I be comfortable holding it 10 years?
members.Charlie Munger: Invert: what would make this investment fail? Am I being influenced by hype, FOMO, or authority bias (just copying someone famous)? If the only reason to buy is 'someone else bought it', say so.
members.Ray Dalio: How does this fit the current macro regime (rates, inflation, geopolitics, war risk)? Does it improve or worsen portfolio diversification across sectors and risk factors?
members.Howard Marks: Where are we in the cycle? Is this priced for perfection? Second-level thinking: what does the market already know, and what is it missing? Risk comes from price paid, not asset quality.
members.Peter Lynch: Do we actually understand what this company does in one sentence? Is growth reasonable for the price (PEG)? Is this a stalwart, fast grower, cyclical, or turnaround — and are we treating it accordingly?
members.Stanley Druckenmiller: Is the risk/reward asymmetric enough to bother? If conviction is genuinely high, is the position too small to matter? And the exit discipline: what specifically would make us cut this fast?

investment_committee.rules: Committee runs AFTER the quality gate, only on remaining buy candidates. One line per member in the trade log. Net-positive vote required. A Munger 'pure hype' rejection counts double.

risk_rules.stop_loss_action: Close any position down 20% or more from cost basis; log which source it came from

committee_member_stats.note: Per-member vote accuracy, graded by the weekly review via the shadow ledger and realized P&L. A member consistently wrong gets their reasoning flagged; a member consistently right gets cited more heavily in close votes.

macro_call_stats.note: Grades the regime classifier: a RISK_OFF/EXTREME call was RIGHT if SPY fell within the following 5 trading days, WRONG if it rose >1%. NORMAL calls graded inversely.

overfitting_guard.rule: No multiplier, threshold, or trust change may be justified by fewer than 5 graded samples. Weekly changes capped at ±0.25. If total closed+graded sample count is under 30, the review must label ALL conclusions 'LOW CONFIDENCE - insufficient data' and make at most one adjustment per week. Chasing noise is the #1 way learning loops destroy strategies.

operational_details.note: Answers to 'how does this break?' — the daily task MUST apply all of these.
operational_details.backup_before_write: Before modifying portfolio.json, copy the current version to portfolio.json.bak. Validate JSON after writing. If corruption detected, restore from .bak and alert Mohammad instead of trading.
operational_details.corporate_action_check: Before executing a stop-loss on a >30% single-day drop, check Tier 1-2 news for a stock split, spin-off, or special dividend. A 2:1 split looks like a 50% crash to a naive system. If corporate action found: adjust shares/cost basis instead of selling.
operational_details.signal_dedupe: Keep 'processed_signals' list in portfolio.json (disclosure fingerprint: politician+ticker+traded_date, or manager+quarter). Never act on the same disclosure twice, even after selling the position.
operational_details.rebuy_cooldown_rule: After a stop-loss or exit-trigger closes a ticker, no new buys of that ticker for 14 days regardless of new signals. Prevents churn loops.
operational_details.dynamic_sizing: Base buy size = 2% of current total portfolio value (not fixed $2,000). Pelosi/boosted sources = base x multiplier. Keeps risk proportional as the portfolio grows or shrinks.
operational_details.macro_data_unavailable: If VIX/market data cannot be fetched, default to RISK_OFF (halve sizes), never to NORMAL. Trading blind deserves caution, not business as usual.
operational_details.instrument_filter: Common stocks only. Skip disclosures of options, bonds, warrants, private placements, and municipal securities. ETFs allowed only if broad-market (SPY/QQQ-class), else skip.
operational_details.earnings_proximity: If the company reports earnings within the next 3 trading days (check Yahoo Finance calendar when available), defer the buy until after the report. Buying into a binary event is gambling, not copying.
operational_details.dividends_simplification: KNOWN SIMPLIFICATION: neither our positions nor the SPY benchmark credit dividends — price-only on both sides, so the comparison stays roughly fair. Revisit if the test goes past 6 months (dividend drag compounds).
